sections.paragraph:
fut. יִטְרֹף, once יִטְרַף Gen 49:27.-

(1) to pluck off (kindred to טָרָה, טָרַם, comp. Gr. θρύπτω), hence Arab. طَرُفَ to be fresh (frifch abgebrochen), Heb. טָרָף, טֶרֶף No. 3.

(2) to pull, tear, in pieces as a wild beast, Gen 37:33 44:28 Deu 33:20 Psal. 22:14 Nah 2:13. Metaph. used of anger, Job 16:9 18:4 and thus even of God, Psal. 50:22 , פֶּן־אֶטְרֹף “lest I tear” like wild beasts. Hos 6:1.

Niphal pass. of No. 2. Exo 22:12 Jer 5:6.

Pual id. Gen 37:33 44:28 .

Hiphil, to tear up food, i.e. to make some one eat, to feed, Pro 30:8. Hence the following words-
